title: Signal Processing and Execution
description: Signal channel polling, signal parsing, action dispatch, semaphore-guarded execution, and source signal handling
activity_flow: ON-DEMAND: processSourceSignal(P partition)  [called by streaming thread]
→ for each matching reader: reader.read() → List<SignalRecord>
→ [has entries] for each entry: split on '.', extract databaseName (parts[0])
→ for each partition in partitionOffsets: matchesDatabase(partition, databaseName)
→ cast to AbstractPartition, compare loggingContext[DATABASE_NAME] case-insensitively
→ [targetPartition present + offset found] return Offsets.of(partition, offset)
→ [targetPartition present + offset missing] WARN, return Offsets.of(Map.of())
→ for each (partition, offset) in finalOffsets: executeSignal(action, signalRecord, jsonData, partition, offset)
→ build SignalPayload(partition, id, type, jsonData, offset, additionalData)
→ submit task to executor: forEach enabledChannelReaders → reader.close()
→ [not shutdown in time] WARN, Thread.interrupted(), shutdownNow(), awaitTermination again
source_signal_channel_flow: Streaming thread receives CDC event from signaling table → SourceSignalChannel.process(Struct value)
→ [other op] config.parseSignallingMessage(value, AFTER) → SignalRecord(id, type, data)
key_decisions: Semaphore guard: tryAcquire(10s) prevents concurrent process() and processSourceSignal() execution
Action lookup: signalActions.get(type) — unknown type logs WARN and skips
Data parse failure: IOException → WARN log, signal skipped
Offset resolution strategy: knownPartition first → single partition shortcut → extract from data-collections → broadcast to all
Database matching: AbstractPartition loggingContext DATABASE_NAME vs data-collection prefix (case-insensitive)
DELETE CDC op on signal table treated as CloseIncrementalSnapshotWindow signal (INSERT_DELETE strategy)

methods: SignalProcessor(connector, config, signalActions, channelReaders, docReader, offsets)

SourceSignalChannel implements SignalCha.note: @NotThreadSafe; used by streaming thread to enqueue, read() called from SignalProcessor thread

concurrent_access_notes: Semaphore(1) guards both process() (scheduler thread) and processSourceSignal() (connector thread) from concurrent execution
partitionOffsets uses ConcurrentHashMap; setContext() may be called from connector thread
SourceSignalChannel.signals is ConcurrentLinkedQueue — safe for concurrent add (streaming) and poll (executor)
test_scenarios: shouldExecuteLog: mock channel returns Log signal → Log action logs message → verified via LogInterceptor
onlyEnabledConnectorShouldExecute: two channels, only generic1 enabled → generic2 signals ignored
shouldIgnoreInvalidSignalType: unknown type → WARN logged, no action executed
shouldIgnoreUnparseableData: malformed JSON data → WARN logged
shouldRegisterAdditionalAction: registerSignalAction('custom', lambda) → custom action executed with payload data
key_relationships: SignalAction receives SignalPayload (carries partition + offset context)
SignalRecord built from CDC Struct via buildSignalRecordFromChangeEventSource
